title: Chapter One
text:
## The Book of the Twos

This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.

“Mendicants, this spiritual life is not lived for the sake of fawning or flattering people, nor for the benefit of possessions, honor, or popularity, nor thinking, ‘So let people know about me!’ This spiritual life is lived for the sake of direct knowledge and complete understanding.” The Buddha spoke this matter. On this it is said:

“The Buddha taught the spiritual life not because of tradition, but for the sake of insight and understanding, and because it culminates in extinguishment.

This is the path followed by the great souls, the great seers. Those who practice it as it was taught by the Buddha doing the teacher’s bidding, make an end of suffering.”

This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.
